confidence_rationale: Yu and Dayan make the most explicit theoretical case in the literature for a clean dissociation between expected uncertainty (tracked by ACh) and unexpected uncertainty (tracked by NE). Unexpected uncertainty -- defined as uncertainty arising from violations of the generative model itself, i.e., the world has changed -- is proposed to drive NE-mediated attentional reorienting. This maps with high fidelity onto MECH-104's claim that unexpected harm (a prime trigger of unexpected uncertainty) elevates NE and enables de-commitment. Confidence docked because this is a computational model with limited direct LC-NE causal data; the ACh/NE dissociation is an inference from behavioral pharmacology rather than direct recording.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Expected uncertainty (known stochasticity within a stable environment) is tracked by acetylcholine, which attenuates prior-driven top-down predictions. Unexpected uncertainty (arising from violations of environmental stability -- model change) is tracked by norepinephrine, which signals that the generative model itself may be wrong and drives attentional reorienting away from current predictions.
mapping.ree_translation: Yu and Dayan's 'unexpected uncertainty' is exactly the regime that unexpected harm events generate in REE: the agent's forward model did not predict harm, so the environment is potentially less stable than represented. NE signalling unexpected uncertainty maps directly onto MECH-104's volatility interrupt: elevated NE from harm-surprise signals that the current commitment (plan) is based on a model that may no longer be valid, providing the formal Bayesian justification for de-commitment. This is the strongest available theoretical grounding for why LC-NE specifically -- rather than dopaminergic prediction error -- should mediate commitment disruption.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Yu and Dayan's model is a normative Bayesian framework inferred from behavioral pharmacology (lesion, muscarinic/nicotinic blockade studies), not direct LC unit recording. The mapping from 'unexpected uncertainty' as a Bayesian quantity to 'commitment uncertainty' as a REE construct requires that (a) NE is implemented by LC specifically (not peripheral sympathetic NE), and (b) the signal reaches commitment-gating circuitry (prefrontal/ACC) in the form postulated. Neither is directly tested in this paper. Additionally, the model does not specify whether the NE signal enables de-commitment by raising threshold for plan continuation or by directly interrupting a committed plan in progress.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical/computational; behavioral pharmacology (rat and human studies cited); no direct electrophysiology

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high: landmark Neuron paper, highly cited, rigorous Bayesian formalisation. Mapping fidelity is the strongest of the four entries -- the 'unexpected uncertainty -> NE -> reorienting' chain maps almost one-to-one onto 'unexpected harm -> LC-NE -> de-commitment'. Transfer risk is moderate: the model is normative and the mechanistic implementation is not fully specified; the step from 'attentional reorienting' to 'commitment de-gating' requires a further architectural argument.
